name: AppHarvest
summary: Kentucky indoor tomato-growing company went public via SPAC in 2021 but filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in January 2024 after years of losses and construction cost overruns.
lesson: Capital-intensive indoor farming requires proven unit economics before scaling to multiple facilities.

name: AeroFarms
summary: Vertical farming pioneer AeroFarms filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in June 2023 after failing to reach profitability despite over a decade of operation and heavy investment.
lesson: Novel growing technology alone cannot overcome persistently negative unit economics.

name: Kalera
summary: Publicly traded vertical farming company Kalera filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in April 2023 and later sold its assets to Sunnyside Fresh.
lesson: Rapid multi-facility expansion without operational profitability accelerates cash burn to insolvency.

name: Fifth Season
summary: Pittsburgh-based robotic vertical farming startup Fifth Season abruptly shut down operations in late 2022, laying off its entire staff.
lesson: Automation-heavy vertical farms still face the same energy-cost economics that sink less automated peers.
source: AgFunder News, Fifth Season shutdown coverage 2022

name: Plenty
summary: SoftBank-backed vertical farming company Plenty, once valued near $1 billion with backing from Jeff Bezos, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in March 2025.
lesson: Even massive venture funding cannot substitute for a path to positive unit economics in indoor farming.

name: Bowery Farming
summary: Once the most valuable vertical farming startup in the US at a $2.3B valuation, Bowery Farming shut down and ceased operations in November 2024 after failing to find a buyer.
lesson: High private valuations do not guarantee a sustainable business model when core unit economics never turn positive.
source: The Information / TechCrunch, Bowery Farming shutdown Nov 2024

name: Infarm
summary: Berlin-based in-store vertical farming company Infarm collapsed in 2023, filing for insolvency in Germany and the Netherlands after mass layoffs and unit withdrawals from retailers.
lesson: Distributed micro-farm networks in retail stores add logistics complexity that can outweigh the benefits of proximity to consumers.

name: CropOne (Crop One Holdings)
summary: Crop One's joint venture with Emirates Flight Catering, Bustanica-precursor Crop One Dubai facility, wound down its original operations in 2022 amid financial strain in the vertical farming sector.
lesson: Vertical farming joint ventures still must clear the same energy and labor cost hurdles as standalone operators.

name: Freight Farms
summary: Container-farm maker Freight Farms, a pioneer of shipping-container hydroponic farms, ceased operations in 2024 after failing to raise further financing.
lesson: Small-format containerized farming struggles to reach the scale economics needed to compete with larger indoor operators.

name: Abundant Robotics
summary: Apple-picking robotics startup Abundant Robotics, spun out of SRI International, ceased operations in late 2021 after struggling to commercialize its harvesting robots at scale.
lesson: Field-robotics products must handle far more variability than lab conditions before they can be commercially deployed profitably.

name: Tortuga AgTech
summary: Strawberry-picking robotics startup Tortuga AgTech shut down in 2022 after failing to reach the reliability and throughput required for commercial field harvesting.
lesson: Agricultural robotics ventures often underestimate how long it takes for perception and manipulation tech to match human harvest speed.

name: PrecisionHawk
summary: Agricultural drone-data pioneer PrecisionHawk wound down its commercial operations and sold off assets in 2023 after years of pivoting business models.
lesson: Repeated business-model pivots in a capital-intensive category can exhaust runway before any version reaches product-market fit.

name: senseFly
summary: Swiss agricultural mapping drone maker senseFly, owned by Parrot, was wound down by its parent company in 2023 amid a broader retreat from the commercial drone market.
lesson: A crowded drone-hardware market with thin margins can make even technically strong products unsustainable for their parent company.

name: 3D Robotics (3DR)
summary: 3D Robotics exited consumer and agricultural drone hardware manufacturing in 2015 after being outcompeted by Chinese manufacturer DJI, pivoting to enterprise drone software instead.
lesson: Hardware startups competing against a vertically integrated low-cost manufacturer need a clear differentiation beyond the airframe itself.

name: Farmigo
summary: Online farmers-market and CSA delivery marketplace Farmigo shut down its consumer marketplace business in 2017 after failing to reach profitability.
lesson: Local food delivery marketplaces face brutal last-mile logistics costs that are hard to offset with typical grocery margins.

name: Good Eggs
summary: Online local-food grocery delivery startup Good Eggs shut down its New York and Los Angeles operations in 2015 and scaled back sharply, with the broader business winding down further by 2019.
lesson: Expanding a low-margin perishable-goods delivery model to multiple cities simultaneously can outrun the operational maturity needed to sustain it.

name: Farmstead
summary: AI-powered online grocery delivery startup Farmstead shut down in 2021 after failing to raise a follow-on funding round.
lesson: Grocery delivery startups without differentiated logistics advantages struggle to raise follow-on capital in a crowded space.

name: Understory
summary: Hyperlocal weather-sensor network startup Understory, which served agricultural insurers and growers, ceased operations around 2022 after failing to scale its sensor network business.
lesson: Physical sensor-network businesses require dense, expensive hardware deployment that can outpace subscription revenue growth.

name: mOasis
summary: Soil-moisture-retaining polymer startup mOasis, which raised funding from prominent agtech investors, shut down around 2019 after its product failed to gain broad grower adoption.
lesson: Agronomic input products need extensive multi-season field validation before growers will pay a premium over established practices.

name: AgBiome
summary: Biological crop-protection company AgBiome laid off most of its staff in 2023 and ceased core operations in 2024 after failing to commercialize its microbial discovery platform at scale.
lesson: Ag-biologicals discovery platforms require very long development timelines that can outlast the patience of venture funding cycles.

name: Benson Hill
summary: Plant-breeding and food ingredient company Benson Hill, which went public via SPAC in 2022, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in July 2024 after mounting losses.
lesson: SPAC mergers can provide capital without fixing an underlying business model that isn't yet generating sustainable margins.
source: Reuters, Benson Hill Chapter 11 filing July 2024

name: Ynsect
summary: French insect-protein unicorn Ynsect underwent major layoffs and restructuring in 2024 after construction delays and cost overruns at its flagship Amiens facility drained its cash reserves.
lesson: Building the world's largest facility for an unproven category multiplies both technical and financial risk simultaneously.

name: Farmers Edge
summary: Canadian precision-agriculture data company Farmers Edge, which went public in 2021, saw its stock collapse and filed for creditor protection under CCAA in 2024.
lesson: Going public does not resolve a data-services business model that hasn't proven it can convert acreage under contract into profit.
source: BNN Bloomberg, Farmers Edge CCAA filing 2024

name: Square Roots
summary: Container-farming and vertical-farm software company Square Roots laid off most staff and shut down its own farming operations in 2023, pivoting to a software-only model.
lesson: Operating physical farms while trying to sell farm-management software to competitors creates conflicting incentives and cost structures.

name: CubicFarm Systems
summary: Publicly traded vertical farming and cattle-feed technology company CubicFarms saw its stock delisted from major exchanges and underwent severe financial distress by 2024.
lesson: Diversifying across multiple unproven agtech product lines can spread limited capital too thin to prove out any single one.

name: FarmWise Labs
summary: Autonomous weeding-robot startup FarmWise laid off a significant portion of its staff in 2023 as it struggled to scale robot deployment profitably across specialty-crop farms.
lesson: Field robotics for niche specialty crops face a smaller addressable market that limits the scale needed to spread hardware R&D costs.

name: Farmcrowdy
summary: Nigerian agtech marketplace Farmcrowdy, once Africa's first Y Combinator-backed agtech startup, laid off most staff and scaled back sharply in 2020 after its crowdfunding-for-farms model failed to scale.
lesson: Agricultural crowdfunding models depend on managing thousands of smallholder farmer relationships, which is far more operationally complex than the fintech layer alone.

name: ThriveAgric
summary: Nigerian agri-fintech ThriveAgric faced a major financial crisis in 2023 amid allegations of fund misuse and an inability to repay investors financing farmer input loans.
lesson: Agri-fintech lending models that pool investor capital against smallholder harvests need rigorous, transparent controls to avoid default cascades.

name: Twiga Foods
summary: Kenyan business-to-business produce marketplace Twiga Foods laid off roughly half its staff in 2023 amid a severe funding crunch and struggled unit economics in its farm-to-retail supply chain.
lesson: Physical produce logistics marketplaces in emerging markets carry thin margins that large funding rounds can mask only temporarily.

name: CompleteFarmer
summary: Ghanaian agtech marketplace CompleteFarmer scaled back operations significantly around 2023 amid broader African agtech funding contraction and unit-economics pressure.
lesson: Early-stage agri-marketplaces in nascent funding ecosystems are highly exposed to broader venture capital pullbacks.
source: TechCabal, African agtech funding downturn coverage 2023

name: Iron Ox
summary: Robotic indoor farming startup Iron Ox laid off a large share of its workforce in 2022-2023 and scaled back its greenhouse-robotics ambitions after failing to hit growth targets.
lesson: Fully autonomous greenhouse robotics adds significant capex on top of the already-thin margins of controlled-environment agriculture.
source: AgFunder News, Iron Ox layoffs coverage 2022-2023

name: Smallhold
summary: Mushroom vertical-farming company Smallhold laid off much of its staff and closed several of its mini-farm facilities in 2023-2024 amid financial distress.
lesson: Distributed in-store micro-farms multiply maintenance and logistics costs relative to the revenue each unit generates.

name: Root AI
summary: AI-powered greenhouse robotics startup Root AI was acquired by AppHarvest in 2021 to bring in-house harvesting robotics and computer vision expertise.
lesson: Being acquired for technology and talent can be a successful outcome for a robotics startup even in a nascent, unproven market.

name: Blue River Technology
summary: Precision spraying and computer-vision weed-detection startup Blue River Technology was acquired by John Deere in 2017 for a reported $305 million.
lesson: Deep-tech precision agriculture startups can achieve strong exits by solving a narrow, high-value problem that a major OEM wants to embed directly into its equipment.
source: John Deere press release, Blue River acquisition 2017

name: Climate Corporation
summary: Weather-data and crop-insurance analytics startup Climate Corporation was acquired by Monsanto in 2013 for approximately $1.1 billion.
lesson: Combining proprietary weather data with actionable risk models can create enough enterprise value to justify a billion-dollar acquisition.
source: Monsanto press release, Climate Corporation acquisition 2013

name: 640 Labs
summary: Farm data-collection hardware startup 640 Labs was acquired by Climate Corporation (then part of Monsanto) in 2014 to expand its in-cab data platform.
lesson: Hardware startups that solve a specific data-collection gap can be attractive tuck-in acquisitions for larger ag data platforms.
source: Climate Corporation press release, 640 Labs acquisition 2014

name: Granular
summary: Farm management software company Granular was acquired by Corteva Agriscience (then DowDuPont) in 2017 for approximately $300 million.
lesson: Farm-management SaaS that integrates cleanly with agronomic decision-making can command a strong premium from agrochemical majors seeking digital platforms.

name: Farmshots
summary: Satellite-imagery crop-monitoring startup Farmshots was acquired by Corteva Agriscience in 2018 to enhance its digital agronomy offerings.
lesson: Remote-sensing analytics startups can find durable exits by plugging into large agrochemical companies' existing distribution channels.

name: Prospera Technologies
summary: AI-based crop-monitoring company Prospera Technologies was acquired by irrigation equipment maker Valmont Industries in 2021.
lesson: Combining computer-vision crop analytics with an established hardware distribution channel like irrigation equipment can validate an AI agtech startup's exit thesis.
source: Valmont Industries press release, Prospera acquisition 2021

name: Bear Flag Robotics
summary: Autonomous tractor-retrofit startup Bear Flag Robotics was acquired by John Deere in 2021 for approximately $250 million to accelerate Deere's autonomy roadmap.
lesson: Autonomy startups with working retrofit kits for existing farm equipment can be acquired quickly by OEMs seeking to compress their own R&D timelines.
source: John Deere press release, Bear Flag Robotics acquisition 2021

name: Vence
summary: Virtual-fencing and livestock-management startup Vence was acquired by Merck Animal Health in 2022 to expand into precision livestock management.
lesson: Virtual fencing solved a real pain point in ranch labor costs, making it an attractive acquisition for an animal-health major entering precision livestock.
source: Merck Animal Health press release, Vence acquisition 2022

name: Cainthus
summary: Facial- and body-recognition livestock analytics startup Cainthus was acquired by Athenium Analytics in 2020 to expand precision livestock monitoring capabilities.
lesson: Applying proven computer-vision techniques to a narrow livestock-monitoring niche can create a clean acquisition thesis for analytics platforms.

name: Trace Genomics
summary: Soil-microbiome genomics testing company Trace Genomics was acquired by Sound Agriculture in 2023 to combine soil diagnostics with biological crop inputs.
lesson: Soil-data companies can achieve successful exits by pairing diagnostic data with a partner that sells the actionable inputs those diagnostics recommend.
source: AgFunder News, Sound Agriculture acquires Trace Genomics 2023

name: Traptic
summary: Robotic strawberry-harvesting startup Traptic was acquired by Swiss agtech robotics firm Ecorobotix in 2023.
lesson: Specialized harvesting robotics ventures can find a successful path to market by merging with a larger robotics platform rather than scaling commercialization alone.

name: ImpactVision
summary: Hyperspectral food-quality imaging startup ImpactVision was acquired by post-harvest coating company Apeel Sciences in 2020.
lesson: Niche imaging technology can find a successful exit when acquired by a larger platform seeking to add quality-detection capability to its supply chain product.

name: CoverCress
summary: Oilseed cover-crop biotech CoverCress, a joint venture, was fully acquired by Bunge and BASF in 2024 to commercialize its low-carbon-intensity crop for biofuel feedstock.
lesson: Novel crop biotech ventures aligned with biofuel feedstock demand can achieve successful full acquisition once commercial-scale planting is proven.

name: Marrone Bio Innovations
summary: Biopesticide company Marrone Bio Innovations, publicly traded since 2013, was acquired by Bioceres Crop Solutions in 2024 after building a commercial biologicals portfolio over nearly two decades.
lesson: Biologicals companies can take many years of patient capital to reach commercial scale, but eventual acquisition can still validate the long path.
source: Bioceres press release, Marrone Bio acquisition 2024

name: Farmers Business Network
summary: Farmer-to-farmer data-sharing and direct-to-farm input marketplace Farmers Business Network has raised nearly $1 billion and continues to operate as an independent company serving tens of thousands of farms.
lesson: Aggregating proprietary farmer data at scale can create durable network effects that support an independent, direct-to-farm input business.

name: DeHaat
summary: Indian full-stack agtech platform DeHaat, offering inputs, advisory, and market access to smallholder farmers, has raised over $300 million and continues to scale across India.
lesson: Full-stack agtech platforms that combine inputs, advice, and output marketing can build strong farmer loyalty in fragmented smallholder markets.

name: Ninjacart
summary: Indian business-to-business fresh-produce supply chain startup Ninjacart has raised over $400 million and operates a large-scale farm-to-retailer logistics network across major Indian cities.
lesson: Solving perishable-goods logistics between farmers and urban retailers at scale can build a durable, high-volume marketplace business.

name: AgroStar
summary: Indian farm-input e-commerce and advisory platform AgroStar has raised over $100 million and continues to expand its farmer customer base across India.
lesson: Combining agronomy advice with an e-commerce input marketplace builds trust that drives repeat farmer engagement.

name: Halter
summary: New Zealand livestock virtual-fencing and monitoring company Halter has raised over $225 million and expanded rapidly across New Zealand and into the United States.
lesson: Solving a clear ranch-labor pain point with reliable hardware can support strong growth and large follow-on funding rounds.

name: Pivot Bio
summary: Nitrogen-fixing microbial products company Pivot Bio has raised over $600 million and scaled commercial sales of its in-field nitrogen products to hundreds of thousands of acres.
lesson: Biologicals that directly replace an expensive, environmentally scrutinized input like synthetic nitrogen can achieve rapid grower adoption.
source: Crunchbase/AgFunder News, Pivot Bio funding rounds

name: Inari Agriculture
summary: Gene-editing seed-technology company Inari Agriculture has raised over $600 million to develop higher-yield, lower-input seed varieties using multiplex gene editing.
lesson: Long seed-development timelines can still attract sustained large-scale venture funding when the underlying yield-improvement thesis is credible.

name: Apeel Sciences
summary: Produce-shelf-life coating company Apeel Sciences has raised over $1 billion, reached unicorn status, and its plant-based coatings are used by major retailers worldwide to reduce food waste.
lesson: A single, clearly measurable value proposition like reduced food waste and spoilage can support very large-scale fundraising and retail adoption.

name: Indigo Agriculture
summary: Microbial seed treatment and carbon-market company Indigo Ag raised over $1.4 billion and, despite major layoffs and leadership changes in 2023, continues to operate its carbon and grain marketplace businesses.
lesson: Even well-funded agtech platforms must eventually narrow their scope to the business lines that show real commercial traction.
source: AgFunder News, Indigo Ag restructuring coverage 2023

name: CropX
summary: Soil-sensor and irrigation-management company CropX has raised over $60 million and continues to operate, serving growers with soil-moisture analytics across multiple countries.
lesson: Durable, field-tested sensor hardware paired with actionable irrigation recommendations can sustain a growing agtech sensor business.

name: Taranis
summary: Aerial crop-intelligence company Taranis has raised over $100 million and continues to provide high-resolution imagery and AI-based pest and disease detection to large-scale growers.
lesson: Combining aerial imagery with AI-based agronomic scouting can create durable enterprise contracts with large grower operations.

name: DroneDeploy
summary: Drone mapping and analytics software company DroneDeploy, widely used in agriculture and construction, reached unicorn status in 2021 and continues to operate at scale.
lesson: Building software-only analytics on top of commodity drone hardware avoids the margin and manufacturing risk that sank many drone hardware makers.

name: Bushel
summary: Grain marketing and farm-management software company Bushel, which merged with FarmLogs in 2020, has raised over $150 million and serves a large share of US grain elevators.
lesson: Consolidating adjacent farm-software products through merger can create a stronger combined platform than either could sustain alone.

name: AgriWebb
summary: Livestock farm-management software company AgriWebb has raised over $40 million and continues to serve livestock producers across Australia, the US, and UK.
lesson: Digitizing livestock record-keeping for a global producer base can sustain steady growth without requiring capital-intensive hardware.

name: Releaf
summary: Nigerian agtech company Releaf, which builds processing technology and financing for palm oil and other crops, has raised several million dollars and continues to scale its manufacturing operations.
lesson: Focusing on a specific crop's processing bottleneck rather than a broad platform can create a more capital-efficient path to scale in emerging markets.

name: Bijak
summary: Indian business-to-business agri-commodity trading platform Bijak has raised over $50 million and continues to facilitate trade between farmers, traders, and processors.
lesson: Digitizing trust and payments in fragmented commodity trading networks can unlock significant transaction volume.

name: Samunnati
summary: Indian agri-value-chain financing company Samunnati has raised over $150 million and continues to provide credit and market linkages to farmer producer organizations.
lesson: Financing the agricultural value chain directly, rather than just farm inputs, can create a durable and less commoditized business model.

name: Local Bounti
summary: Publicly traded controlled-environment agriculture company Local Bounti, which went public via SPAC in 2022, underwent a severe debt restructuring in 2024 to avoid bankruptcy after mounting losses.
lesson: SPAC-funded agtech companies still face the same fundamental margin pressures as their privately held peers.

name: Gotham Greens
summary: Greenhouse-grown produce company Gotham Greens has raised over $440 million and operates profitable greenhouse facilities across the US, distinguishing itself from failed vertical-farming peers.
lesson: Traditional sunlight-based greenhouse growing can be a more capital-efficient controlled-environment model than fully artificial-light vertical farms.
source: Crunchbase/AgFunder News, Gotham Greens funding history

name: BrightFarms
summary: Greenhouse-grown packaged salad company BrightFarms was acquired by Cox Enterprises in 2021, giving the greenhouse operator additional capital to expand its retail distribution network.
lesson: Greenhouse produce companies with established retail relationships can find successful exits with strategic acquirers seeking food and agriculture diversification.
source: AgFunder News, Cox Enterprises acquires BrightFarms 2021

name: Solum
summary: Soil and tissue testing analytics company Solum was acquired by AGCO Corporation in 2017 to expand AGCO's precision agriculture services offering.
lesson: Rapid soil-testing technology can find a strong exit path by feeding directly into an equipment maker's precision agronomy services.

name: Farmobile
summary: Farm data-capture and ownership platform Farmobile was acquired by AGCO Corporation in 2022 to strengthen AGCO's data services and precision agriculture offerings.
lesson: Building a farmer-owned data model rather than platform-owned data can differentiate a startup enough to attract a large OEM acquirer.

name: Sound Agriculture
summary: Molecule-discovery agtech company Sound Agriculture has raised over $165 million and continues to sell its nutrient-efficiency products commercially to row-crop farmers.
lesson: Small-molecule agricultural chemistry that improves existing nutrient uptake can reach commercial sales faster than novel biologics platforms.
source: Crunchbase/AgFunder News, Sound Agriculture funding history

name: Advanced Farm Technologies
summary: Robotic strawberry-harvesting company Advanced Farm Technologies has raised over $85 million and continues to commercially deploy its harvesting robots with growers.
lesson: Sustained, well-capitalized iteration on harvesting robotics can eventually reach commercial viability where earlier competitors failed.
source: Crunchbase/AgFunder News, Advanced Farm Technologies funding history

name: Semios
summary: Precision agriculture sensor and pest-management network Semios has raised over $100 million and continues to serve tree-fruit and nut growers across North America.
lesson: Deep specialization in a specific crop segment's pest-management needs can sustain a durable sensor-network business.

name: AgroFresh
summary: Post-harvest freshness technology company AgroFresh, spun off from Dow Chemical, has traded publicly since 2015 and remains a market leader in produce shelf-life extension technology.
lesson: A proven, patented post-harvest chemistry with an established customer base can sustain an independent public company for decades.
source: AgroFresh SEC filings and investor relations

name: Arable Labs
summary: In-field crop-monitoring hardware and analytics company Arable Labs has raised over $40 million and continues to serve commercial growers and agronomic researchers.
lesson: Combining weather, soil, and canopy sensing in a single field device can create a defensible niche versus broader precision-ag platforms.

name: Full Harvest
summary: Business-to-business marketplace for imperfect and surplus produce Full Harvest has raised over $36 million and continues to connect growers with food and beverage manufacturers.
lesson: Marketplaces that monetize existing food waste streams can build durable supply relationships without needing to control physical assets.

name: Rantizo
summary: Agricultural drone-spraying company Rantizo has raised over $25 million and built a network of certified applicators using drones for precision crop-protection spraying across the US.
lesson: Focusing on drone application services rather than drone hardware manufacturing avoids the margin compression that sank many ag-drone hardware makers.

name: Concentric Ag (formerly Agrible)
summary: Agronomic decision-support company Agrible was acquired by Nutrien in 2018, and its team later formed Concentric Ag, continuing to build sustainability and carbon-program software for agriculture.
lesson: Founders whose first agtech company gets acquired successfully often reuse that domain expertise to found new, similarly successful ventures.

name: Tridge
summary: Global agricultural trade intelligence and marketplace platform Tridge has raised over $100 million and reached unicorn valuation while connecting agri-food buyers and suppliers worldwide.
lesson: Aggregating opaque global agricultural trade data into a searchable platform can create strong network effects among buyers and suppliers.

name: WayCool Foods
summary: Indian agri-supply-chain and food brands company WayCool Foods has raised over $180 million and continues to operate its farm-to-retail distribution network across southern India.
lesson: Building owned brands on top of a produce supply chain can create additional margin beyond pure logistics fees.

name: Trringo
summary: Farm equipment rental marketplace Trringo, a Mahindra Group venture, continues to operate profitably across India, giving smallholder farmers access to tractors and machinery on demand.
lesson: An equipment-rental marketplace model can reach smallholder farmers who cannot afford to own machinery outright, without the capital intensity of owning the whole fleet.

name: CoBank/AgriDigital blockchain grain platform
summary: Blockchain-based grain commodity management platform AgriDigital scaled back significantly around 2021 after failing to achieve broad industry adoption of its blockchain settlement layer.
lesson: Blockchain-based supply chain tools often struggle when the underlying industry lacks the digital-trust problem the technology is meant to solve.

name: Ripe.io
summary: Blockchain-based produce traceability startup Ripe.io scaled back operations significantly around 2022 after failing to convert pilot programs with retailers into recurring commercial contracts.
lesson: Traceability startups need a clear paying customer for the data, not just a compelling blockchain narrative, to sustain commercial contracts.

name: Sundrop Farms
summary: Solar-powered desalination greenhouse company Sundrop Farms, after building large facilities in Australia and the US, faced severe financial distress and restructuring around 2020.
lesson: Combining novel renewable-energy infrastructure with greenhouse agriculture multiplies capital risk across two unproven cost curves at once.
source: AgFunder News, Sundrop Farms financial distress coverage

name: Element Robotics / Agrist
summary: Early-stage harvesting robotics venture Element Robotics wound down around 2023 after its autonomous picking hardware failed to reach commercial reliability targets.
lesson: Harvesting robots that cannot consistently match human-level speed and accuracy in variable field conditions will struggle to attract paying grower customers.

name: Harvest CROO Robotics
summary: Strawberry-picking robot developer Harvest CROO Robotics faced major funding shortfalls and slowed development significantly by 2023 after years of delayed commercial deployment.
lesson: Complex multi-armed harvesting robots for delicate fruit require far longer and more expensive development cycles than initial timelines suggest.
source: AgFunder News, Harvest CROO Robotics coverage

name: Verdant Robotics
summary: Precision agricultural robotics company Verdant Robotics has raised over $56 million and continues to commercially deploy its multi-action robots for weeding and precision spraying.
lesson: Combining multiple precision actions in one robotic platform can improve the economics enough to reach commercial adoption where single-purpose robots struggled.
source: Crunchbase/AgFunder News, Verdant Robotics funding history

name: Connecterra
summary: AI-based dairy cattle health and productivity monitoring company Connecterra has raised over $18 million and continues to serve dairy farms across multiple countries.
lesson: A focused sensor-and-AI product for a single, high-value livestock segment like dairy can sustain steady growth without needing a broad platform play.

name: Freshworks Studio (Farmshelf)
summary: Small-scale in-store and in-restaurant vertical farming unit maker Farmshelf ceased independent operations around 2022 after failing to scale beyond niche hospitality installations.
lesson: Micro-scale vertical farming units serving niche hospitality customers rarely generate enough volume to sustain a standalone hardware company.

name: Nutrien Ag Solutions digital retail (Agrible integration)
summary: Agronomic modeling company Agrible was acquired by Nutrien in 2018 and successfully integrated into Nutrien's digital agriculture retail offerings still in use today.
lesson: Agronomic modeling startups can achieve durable success when their technology is absorbed into a retailer's ongoing grower-facing digital services.

name: Pairwise
summary: Gene-editing produce company Pairwise has raised over $275 million and brought its first CRISPR-edited leafy greens product to US retail shelves in 2023.
lesson: Reaching an actual retail product launch, even years after founding, validates a gene-editing platform's ability to convert R&D into a commercial good.

name: Farmshare Inc.
summary: Community-supported agriculture management platform Farmshare shut down around 2019 after failing to build a sustainable subscription revenue base among small farms.
lesson: Software serving small, low-budget farm operations often cannot generate enough per-customer revenue to sustain a dedicated venture-backed company.

name: AeroVironment agricultural drone unit
summary: AeroVironment's agricultural imaging drone product line was discontinued around 2021 as the company refocused entirely on defense-sector drone contracts.
lesson: Diversified drone manufacturers often exit lower-margin agricultural applications when higher-margin defense contracts offer a better return on R&D.
source: Industry press, AeroVironment product line coverage

name: Vertical Field
summary: Modular container-based vertical farming company Vertical Field has raised over $45 million and continues to deploy its smart soil-based container farms at retail and community sites globally.
lesson: Using soil-based growing rather than fully hydroponic systems can lower energy costs enough to sustain a container-farming business where hydroponic peers failed.
source: Crunchbase/AgFunder News, Vertical Field funding history

name: Agreena
summary: Soil carbon-farming and carbon-credit marketplace Agreena has raised over $56 million and continues to expand its regenerative-agriculture carbon program across Europe.
lesson: Building the measurement and marketplace infrastructure for agricultural carbon credits early can position a company well as regulatory demand grows.

name: DroneSeed
summary: Reforestation drone company DroneSeed has raised over $36 million and continues to operate aerial seeding and post-fire reforestation services for forestry and timber customers.
lesson: Applying agricultural drone technology to a specific, underserved niche like post-wildfire reforestation can avoid the commoditized crop-imaging market that many drone startups struggled in.
